title: 5 Ordering Canadian Dollar Money Orders and Controlling Stock
body_text:
5	Ordering Canadian Dollar Money Orders and Controlling Stock
5.1	Overview of Ordering Canadian Dollar Money Orders and Controlling Stock
Canadian dollar Money Orders are printed and packaged upon request and then shipped to the credit union. Neither Westkey Graphics nor D+H maintains an inventory of printed Money Orders. Therefore, it is important to order a supply of Money Orders a few weeks in advance of when they are required.
The Money Orders must be shipped to the credit union by armoured car. To minimize delivery costs, the credit union can arrange to have Money Orders delivered on the same day that the armoured car delivers cash parcels to the credit union.
Westkey Graphics and D+H supply Canadian dollar Money Orders that meet the specifications of CPA Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items.
5.2	Ordering Canadian Dollar Money Orders (BC)
This section applies to BC credit unions.
To order a supply of Canadian dollar Money Orders, phone, fax, or email your order to Westkey Graphics.
Note:	Each Money Order has a maximum face value of $500 and the minimum order is 500 items.
Money Orders are printed and packaged within ten business days. The delivery date depends on when the credit unionâs armoured car service picks up the Money Orders. The Money Order supplier will coordinate shipment of the Money Orders with the credit unionâs armoured car company.
Westkey Graphics invoices the credit union for the cost of the Money Order supply. The value of a Money Order is charged to the credit union when the item is presented for payment or cashed and settled through the Clearing.
5.3	Ordering Canadian Dollar Money Orders (Ontario)
This section applies to Ontario credit unions.
Proceed as follows to order a supply of Canadian dollar Money Orders:
Complete the D+H Personal Money Orders â Order Form. See Figure 1.
Note:	Each Money Order has a maximum face value of $1,000 and the minimum order is 200 items.
Figure 1:  D+H Personal Money Orders â Order Form
Fax or email the form to D+H as directed on the form.
Money Order requests received by Tuesday at 12:00 pm ET will be available for pickup at D+H by Friday at 9:00 am ET. The delivery date depends on when the credit unionâs armoured car service picks up the Money Orders. Credit unions are responsible for contacting their armoured car company to arrange pickup of Money Orders at D+H.
D+H invoices the credit union for the cost of the Money Order supply. The value of a Money Order is charged to the credit union when the item is presented for payment or cashed and settled through the Clearing.
5.4	Controlling Credit Union Stock of Money Orders
Money Orders are like cash and must be treated as such. Establish an internal procedure that includes the following steps:
Receive incoming shipments of Money Orders in joint custody and enter the information into the vault register. Check that the number of Money Orders received corresponds to the number that was ordered and note the serial numbers.
If applicable, maintain a log of Money Orders issued to each branch.
Keep the main supply of Money Orders in the vault with a small working supply at the front counter.
Record the serial numbers of Money Orders issued to each teller.
Compare the physical inventory of Money Orders to the vault register on a regular basis (e.g. monthly). Ensure that access to the vault register and the supply of blank Money Orders is restricted, under joint custody, to appointed custodians.
Issue Money Orders in numerical order.
Donât destroy voided Money Orders. See Section 7.2, Voiding Canadian Dollar Money Orders.
Related Information
Section 7.2
, Voiding Canadian Dollar Money Orders
5.5	Insurance of Money Order Stock
Canadian dollar Money Orders are drawn directly on the credit union and are insured under separate limits under the Master Bond Program. This applies to credit unions in BC and Ontario.
END
